ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term specific to chocolate-filled snack pie; broader 'pie (food product)' used as nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP 'Pastries and cakes' is the closest defensible category for a chocolate-filled sweet pastry snack; no exact code for chocolate snack pie exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept identified for chocolate snack pie.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chocolate pie piece weighs approximately 35 g, representing a single individually wrapped snack serving as commonly sold in Singapore convenience stores and supermarkets.

nutrient_source.title: Chocolate Pie — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chocolate Pie (cr_id F11080010). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and sugar values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry 'Pie, chocolate creme, commercially prepared' (FDC ID 172152) as HPB does not report these sub-fractions; this is documented as an adapted estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sweet Biscuits and Pastry Products — analogous estimate based on chocolate-filled biscuit/pastry category
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for this specific HPB chocolate pie product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate based on chocolate-filled sweet pastry / biscuit entries in the University of Sydney GI Database (e.g., chocolate-filled biscuit snacks, GI range 35–45). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 60.49 × 0.35) / 100 = 8.5. Despite the low GI, the high carbohydrate density and zero dietary fibre make this product clinically concerning for glycaemic management.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (~28 g/100 g), borderline sodium (366 mg/100 g), and high saturated fat (~8.9 g/100 g) combined with zero dietary fibre
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid chocolate pie; the combination of high free sugars (28 g/100 g), borderline sodium (366 mg/100 g), and high saturated fat independently elevates blood pressure risk, and zero dietary fibre provides no mitigating effect on glucose or lipid absorption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high refined carbohydrate (60.5 g/100 g) with estimated sugar ~28 g/100 g and zero dietary fibre, producing rapid postprandial glucose excursions despite a low measured GI
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid chocolate pie entirely; the estimated sugar content of 28 g/100 g exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling, and the complete absence of dietary fibre means there is no modulation of glucose absorption, making this product unsuitable for blood glucose management regardless of its low GI label.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~8.9 g/100 g), estimated trans fat (~0.3 g/100 g from vegetable shortening), high free sugars (~28 g/100 g), and borderline sodium (366 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid chocolate pie; the estimated trans fat (~0.3 g/100 g from partially hydrogenated vegetable shortening) and high saturated fat (~8.9 g/100 g) raise LDL cholesterol and promote arterial inflammation, while high free sugars and borderline sodium further compound cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chocolate pie is a commercially produced ultra-processed sweet snack consisting of a soft baked wheat-flour pastry shell filled with a chocolate-flavoured cream. It is widely sold in Singapore and across Southeast Asia in individually wrapped single-serve portions of approximately 35 g. The texture is soft and slightly chewy with a sweet, cocoa-rich filling. The product is energy-dense at 444 kcal per 100 g, driven by high refined carbohydrate (60.5 g/100 g), moderate-to-high fat (19.3 g/100 g including approximately 8.9 g saturated fat and an estimated 0.3 g trans fat from vegetable shortening), and an estimated free sugar content of approximately 28 g/100 g. Dietary fibre is absent (0 g/100 g), meaning there is no buffering of glucose absorption. Sodium is 366 mg/100 g, placing it in the borderline range for hypertension management. Despite a low measured glycemic index of approximately 40 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for chocolate-filled pastry snacks), the high carbohydrate density and zero fibre make this product clinically unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The product is vegetarian and typically halal-certified in Singapore. It is not suitable for vegan, gluten-free, or dairy-free diets. Common allergens include gluten, milk, eggs, and soy.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a chocolate pie?
Is chocolate pie suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of chocolate pie?
Is chocolate pie high in fat?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11080010, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, trans fat, and sugar are adapted estimates from USDA FoodData Central 'Pie, chocolate creme, commercially prepared' (FDC ID 172152), which is the closest approved-source analogue. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for chocolate-filled pastry snacks. Overall confidence is high given the primary HPB source match and well-supported secondary adaptations.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source (F11080010); this is a refined ultra-processed product with no added fibre by formulation — confirmed zero per HPB FoodData. 2. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 in the HPB source; these are confirmed zero per HPB FoodData for this product. 3. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific commercial chocolate pie product are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11080010) or USDA FoodData Central for the closest analogue as of 2026-04-16. 4. saturated_fat_g (8.9 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (7.2 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (2.1 g), trans_fat_g (0.3 g), and sugar_g (28.0 g) are adapted estimates from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 172152 'Pie, chocolate creme, commercially prepared' as HPB does not report these sub-fractions for this entry. 5. GI value of 40 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for chocolate-filled sweet pastry/biscuit snacks (GI range 35–45); no direct measurement exists for this specific product. 6. Halal status is assumed based on typical commercial chocolate pie products sold in Singapore; consumers should verify packaging certification. 7. Trans fat from partially hydrogenated vegetable shortening is a clinical concern for cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis; consumers should check ingredient labels for 'partially hydrogenated oils'.
